definition: The analytical separation of yield curve movements into expectations-driven and term-premium-driven components. Raw 2s10s spread is insufficient and often misleading; decomposition is required to correctly interpret the curve's economic signal.
evidence: 2019 expectations-driven inversion (low MOVE) correctly signaled slowdown; 2022-23 term-premium-contaminated inversion (high MOVE) did not produce recession on historical schedule
Equity markets rallied 25%+ through the 2022-23 inversion, inconsistent with genuine recession signal
CLO issuance dropped 35% YoY in 2022 when term premium dynamics shifted—curve shape alone did not predict this
Vayanos-Vila (2021) provides theoretical grounding for the decomposition framework
open_questions: Can term premium be measured with sufficient real-time precision to be actionable given 30-50bp model divergence?
Is there a model-free or semi-parametric estimator using TIPS breakevens, swaption vols, and survey data to cross-check ACM/KW?
